info.title: dotnet-bld
info.description: Tool to clean build output folders for msbuild, dotnet build

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: clean
commands.description: Cleans solution / project build output (bin/obj etc.)

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --non-current
options.description: Only clean directories for non-current target frameworks. [default: False]

options.name: --obj
options.description: Also clean BaseIntermediateOutputPath (obj folder). [default: False]

options.name: --keep-assets
options.description: When cleaning obj, preserve NuGet restore artifacts (project.assets.json etc.) and only delete build output subdirectories. [default: False]

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --output-file
options.description: Path to the output file. [default: clean.sh]

options.name: --force
options.description: Do not ask for confirmation (requires explicit root). [default: False]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]

options.name: --delete
options.description: Actually delete files. [default: False]

options.name: --confirm
options.description: Confirmation Level for deletion. [default: Directory]

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --non-current
options.description: Only clean directories for non-current target frameworks. [default: False]

options.name: --obj
options.description: Also clean BaseIntermediateOutputPath (obj folder). [default: False]

options.name: --keep-assets
options.description: When cleaning obj, preserve NuGet restore artifacts (project.assets.json etc.) and only delete build output subdirectories. [default: False]

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]

commands.name: nuget
commands.description: Analyze and categorize NuGet package references in projects.

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]

options.name: --whitelist-blacklist-file
options.description: Path to the whitelist/blacklist file containing package filtering rules.

options.name: --aggregate
options.description: Display packages aggregated across all projects instead of per-project view. [default: True]

options.name: --show-projects
options.description: In aggregate mode, show which projects reference each package (default: true). [default: True]

commands.name: containerize
commands.description: Analyze Dockerfiles and .NET projects with SDK container build properties (PublishProfile=DefaultContainer, ContainerBaseImage, or ContainerImage).

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --list
options.description: Only list Dockerfiles without parsing details. [default: False]

options.name: --projects
options.description: Scan for .NET projects with container build properties. [default: False]

options.name: --all
options.description: Scan for both Dockerfiles and .NET container projects. [default: False]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]

commands.name: cpm
commands.description: Convert all projects in a solution to Central Package Management.

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --apply
options.description: Apply changes to create Directory.Packages.props and update project files. [default: False]

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: Overwrite existing Directory.Packages.props if it exists. [default: False]

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]

commands.name: outdated
commands.description: Check for outdated NuGet packages and optionally update them to latest versions.

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --apply
options.description: Apply package updates instead of just checking. [default: False]

options.name: --skip-tfm-check
options.description: Skip target framework compatibility checking when suggesting package updates. [default: False]

options.name: --prerelease
options.description: Include prerelease versions of NuGet packages. [default: False]

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --from
options.description: Source target framework(s) (e.g., net8.0 or net8.0,net9.0 for multiple). If not specified, will be auto-detected from project files.

options.name: --to
options.description: Target framework to migrate to (e.g., net9.0).

options.name: --apply
options.description: Apply changes (default is dry-run). [default: False]

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]

commands.name: build-props
commands.description: Analyze Directory.Build.props files and property provenance across projects. (BETA)

options.name: --markdown
options.description: Emit markdown table output where supported. [default: False]

options.name: --root
options.description: Root directory, .sln, or project file. Can also be specified as trailing argument.

options.name: --depth
options.description: If root is a directory, recursion depth to search for .sln or project files. [default: 3]

options.name: --list
options.description: Only list imported Directory.Build.props files in a tree structure, without showing property contents. [default: False]

options.name: --no-overridden
options.description: Hide properties that originate from Directory.Build.props but are overridden later in evaluation. [default: False]

options.name: --properties
options.description: Comma-separated list of properties to trace (e.g., TargetFramework,Nullable). When specified, only shows these properties.

options.name: --log
options.description: Log verbosity (Debug, Verbose, Info, Warning, Error). [default: Warning]

options.name: --vstoolspath
options.description: Explicit value for VSToolsPath. If not specified, the tool tries to resolve VSToolsPath either from environment variable or via vswhere.exe. VSToolsPath contains additional msbuild target files which may be required for evaluation of project files.

options.name: --novstoolspath
options.description: Do not try to resolve VSToolsPath from ENV or vswhere.exe. [default: False]

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Use parallel processing for project evaluation. [default: True]

options.name: --concurrency
options.description: Degree of parallelism for project evaluation. [default: 2]
